signals.reasoning: BTC is recovering well, but there is still no unique edge here relative to the cleaner alt squeeze setups. It stays context, not trade.
ETH has improved with the tape, but the move still looks like broad recovery rather than a standout fresh signal. Better to stay neutral.
SOL is stronger than it was, but the board offers cleaner expressions than chasing a major that has already recovered sharply. No upgrade here.
TST has become too extended after the rebound. Even though momentum is strong, the reward-to-risk on a fresh long has deteriorated too much to justify chasing.
STABLE still has one of the cleanest squeeze setups on the board with strongly negative funding and a constructive recovery structure. It remains the best reduced-size long.
CHILLGUY is constructive, but the move still reads more like recovery than a clean new breakout entry. It is interesting, just not clean enough yet.
WCT has supportive trend and open-interest behavior, but the move is already far enough along and execution quality is still messy enough that I do not want to chase it.
BOME is still too thin and too noisy to trust as a fresh trade. The chart can move fast, but the edge is not clean.
ZEREBRO still looks more like a short-covering rebound than a durable fresh long. High beta after the first recovery leg makes it too easy to get trapped.
STBL has recovered strongly, but it now sits in an awkward middle ground where the bullish recovery is real while funding is no longer friendly enough for a fresh chase. Neutral is cleaner.
GOAT remains damaged, but the downside is already mature enough that it does not qualify as a clean fresh short. Weak structure alone is still not enough.
